vulnerability.title: Add nosuid Option to /boot
vulnerability.description: The nosuid mount option can be used to prevent execution of setuid programs in /boot. The SUID and SGID permissions should not be required on the boot partition. Add the nosuid option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /boot.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /boot has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/boot")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point '/boot' is not even in /etc/fstab, so we can't set up mount options" >&2; echo "Not

triage_decision.reason: Adding nosuid to /boot is a standard security hardening measure that prevents setuid program execution on the boot partition, which is not required for normal operations. The remediation includes checks to ensure /boot is defined in /etc/fstab and avoids certain platforms, minimizing risk.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Security hardening; Prevents setuid execution on /boot

execution_details.stdout:
#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info.
#
# After editing this file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' to update systemd
# units generated from this file.
#
/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults,nodev        0 0
UUID=F56C-228D /boot/efi vfat umask=0077,shortname=winnt,nosuid 0 2
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs defaults 0 0

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The user wants me to remediate an OpenSCAP finding. The finding is about adding the "nosuid" option to the /boot mount point in /etc/fstab.

Let me understand the task:
- I need to add "nosuid" to the mount options for /boot in /etc/fstab
- The rule is: mount_option_boot_nosuid
- Severity: 2 (medium)
- I should use ONLY the provided tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file
- I must follow the workflow: PLAN -> REVIEW -> APPLY

My approach:
1. First, I'll read the /etc/fstab file to see the current configuration for /boot
2. Then I'll determine how to add "nosuid" to the mount options
3. The options are in the fourth column (separated by commas)
4. I need to be careful not to duplicate if it's already there
5. I'll use sed to modify the line in-place

Let me start by reading /etc/fstab to see the current state.
reasoning_messages.content: I'll help you remediate this OpenSCAP finding by adding the nosuid option to /boot in /etc/fstab. Let me start by examining the current configuration.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The /etc/fstab file shows that /boot is already defined with an xfs filesystem and currently has "defaults,nodev" options. I need to add "nosuid" to the mount options. The line is:

UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs defaults,nodev 0 0

I need to change this to:

UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs defaults,nodev,nosuid 0 0

Or I could change it to defaults,nodev,nosuid. Let me use sed to modify this in-place.

I'll craft a sed command that will add nosuid to the options field. The options are in the fourth column (mount options). I need to be careful to preserve the existing options and just add nosuid.

Let me first review my plan with the reviewer.
